tuning_summary.main_bottleneck: Small M dimension (512) limits parallelism and makes WMMA overhead significant
approaches_tried: Smaller 64x64 tiles (1.12 TFLOPS, poor arithmetic intensity)
Shared memory swizzling (1.24 TFLOPS, memory bound)
Split-K with atomics (0.93 TFLOPS, atomic contention)
BK=32/64 variations (worse due to smem pressure)
recommendations: Consider using cuBLAS for this specific shape
Explore stream-K or persistent kernel approaches
Try CuTe/CUTLASS for optimized tile scheduling
